institution: Sector-wide typology - Indian retail digital payments
source_documents: https://www.rbi.org.in (Master Direction - Digital Payment Security Controls, Feb 2021)
category: Card-not-present (CNP) fraud - use of stolen card data online
risk_signals: Small 'testing' authorisations followed by a large purchase (card-testing pattern)
High-velocity attempts across many cards from one device/IP, or sequential BIN attacks
Mismatch between billing and shipping details, or shipping to a freight-forwarder
analyst_reasoning: With stolen PAN/expiry/CVV, fraudsters transact online where no physical card is needed. They first validate cards with tiny charges, then monetise with larger purchases. The signatures are velocity, card-testing, and billing/shipping mismatches rather than any single transaction looking wrong.
investigation_steps: Detect card-testing: clusters of small authorisations preceding a large charge
Measure velocity and BIN-attack patterns per device fingerprint and IP
Flag billing/shipping mismatches and reship/freight-forwarder destinations
decision: Decline and step-up (3-D Secure / OTP) on risk-scored transactions, block offending device/IP, and push tokenization for stored cards.
outcome: Successful CNP fraud results in chargebacks to the merchant; tokenization and 2-factor step-up sharply reduce it.
lessons_learned: Card-testing micro-authorisations are an early indicator of a compromised-card run
Velocity and billing/shipping mismatch are stronger signals than any single transaction
Tokenization and 3-D Secure are the structural defences against CNP fraud
